key_insight: Pareto fronts in multiobjective topology optimization can be assembled from local fronts, leading to disconnected or nonconvex trade-off curves, and scalarization choice strongly affects how well they are sampled.
engineering_relevance: For design problems balancing criteria like compliance, volume, stress, and dynamic response, this work clarifies which scalarization strategies yield more uniform and reliable approximations of the true trade-off set.
method: Compared weighted-sum and ε-constraint methods against Pascoletti-Serafini scalarization.
Analyzed the structure of Pareto fronts in topology optimization, showing they may consist of local-front segments.
Performed numerical bi-objective studies across volume, compliance, maximum stress, and dynamic compliance examples.
evidence_signal: The paper reports a large set of bi-objective topology optimization examples and uses them to compare front coverage and clustering behavior across scalarization methods.
watch_item: Weighted-sum approaches can miss nonconvex or disconnected regions of the Pareto front, so relying on them alone may underrepresent feasible design trade-offs.
paper_title: Novel insights into Pareto fronts in multiobjective topology optimization and a comparative study of scalarization strategies
